item.title: Raman defeats Pratt to face Bass in LA mayoral election, CBS News projects
item.description: CBS News projects that incumbent Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass will face off against Nithya Raman in November's runoff election.

item.title: Trump NBA Finals visit locks down 5-block perimeter around Madison Square Garden
item.description: President Trump will watch the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs in Game 3 of the NBA Finals from an executive suite, as an invited guest of Madison Square Garden CEO James Dolan. Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: Details on extreme security restrictions over Trump's NBA Finals visit
item.description: President Trump will be in attendance Monday night for Game 3 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden. CBS News' Anna Schecter has details on the security surrounding the visit.

item.title: Inside the astronomical ticket prices for sporting events and concerts
item.description: The average cost on the secondary market for a ticket to Game 3 of the NBA Finals between the Knicks and Spurs at Madison Square Garden is over $8,000. That's up from around $3,000 when they first went on sale on May 27. Joe Enoch, CBS News consumer, business and technology reporter Joe Enoch has more on the high ticket costs across the entertainment industry.

item.title: U.S. bars entry of FIFA World Cup referee from Somalia
item.description: Federal immigration authorities barred a Somali soccer referee who was slated to officiate the FIFA World Cup from entering the U.S. over the weekend, citing "vetting concerns."

item.title: South Florida feels powerful earthquake off Cuba
item.description: An earthquake off Cuba was felt across parts of Florida, but Miami officials reported no major injuries, significant damage or tsunami threat.

item.title: Former NY Knick, NJ Sen. Bill Bradley says Trump "second fiddle" at Game 3
item.description: "The people are there to see these two teams play," Bill Bradley said of President Trump's plan to attend Game 3 of the NBA Finals on Monday night.

item.title: Maine voters voice support for Platner on eve of primary despite controversies
item.description: Democratic voters in Maine are still voicing support for Graham Platner despite the string of scandals that have rocked his campaign for U.S. Senate. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns reports from Maine a day ahead of the primary election there.

item.title: 5 people stabbed at Penn Station, Amtrak police say
item.description: Six people, including the suspect, were taken to the hospital after a stabbing incident at New York City's Penn Station on Sunday evening.

item.title: How to buy SpaceX shares as its blockbuster IPO readies for liftoff
item.description: SpaceX is setting aside a large chunk of shares for ordinary investors as it seeks to raise a record $75 billion. Here's what to know.

item.title: OpenAI says it filed confidential IPO as it positions itself for AI arms race
item.description: Going public will allow OpenAI, valued at $852 billion, to inject more cash into its business as the AI race quickens.

item.title: Former Sen. Bill Bradley on Trump visit to Finals game: "He's second fiddle"
item.description: Former Sen. Bill Bradley won two NBA championships as a player with the New York Knicks in 1970 and 1973. He joined CBS News to discuss President Trump's planned visit to Madison Square Garden for Game 3 of the NBA Finals Monday night.

item.title: Deadly Philippines earthquake unleashes small tsunami
item.description: A deadly earthquake rocked the southern Philippines, killing dozens of people and sending small tsunami waves toward at least three nations.

item.title: Prediction markets have become a draw for young men. Here's why
item.description: Prediction markets have become a draw for young men in search of quick cash and thrills, experts say. "I had almost $4,600 at one point but squandered that," one man said.

item.title: Where things stand in the Middle East after Iran and Israel suspend first attacks since ceasefire
item.description: Iran and Israel said Monday that they had halted military operations after exchanging their first attacks since a ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran came into effect in early April. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Joe Zacks have more.

item.title: Kid who's been posting prayers for Knicks on Finals run: "It feels really, really good"
item.description: Kobe, a 7-year-old, has been posting prayers online for the New York Knicks for their last 13 games. They haven't lost a game in that stretch and are now up 2-0 on the San Antonio Spurs in the NBA Finals. Kobe and his mom, Tiffany Smith, joined CBS News to discuss the run.

item.title: Trump formally nominates Todd Blanche as attorney general
item.description: Currently acting attorney general, Todd Blanche may face an uphill confirmation battle from some wary Republican senators.

item.title: Flooding could impact more than 88 million as storms threaten multiple states
item.description: States across the Midwest and northern Plains could see flooding as severe storms hit, forecasters say.

item.title: Judge voids Trump's $100,000 fee for new H-1B visas
item.description: U.S. District Judge Leo Sorokin ruled in favor of a group of 20 states that challenged President Trump's new $100,000 visa fee.

item.title: Iran and Israel say attacks halted after Trump tells both to "stop 'shooting'"
item.description: Iran and Israel declare a halt to fighting as President Trump says both are seeking an "immediate ceasefire" after a major escalation in the 101-day war.

item.title: Can an AI agent cover for you while you're at the beach?
item.description: "AI can make everything that was on my plate visible to colleagues while I'm gone," one expert said.

item.title: A timeline of the Karen Read case
item.description: A look at the timeline of events in Karen Read's high-profile Massachusetts murder trial and retrial. Read was acquitted of killing her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O'Keefe.

item.title: Trump NBA Finals visit forces extreme NYC security restrictions, watch party canceled
item.description: President Trump will be attending Game 3 of the NBA Finals on Monday night, which has forced major security restrictions near New York City's Madison Square Garden. CBS News reporter Jared Ochacher has more.

item.title: Working remotely can hurt people's mental health, research finds
item.description: Although working remotely offers flexibility, it also takes a toll on people's mental health, experts said.

item.title: Trump walks out of interview with NBC
item.description: President Trump abruptly ended an interview with NBC News when repeatedly challenged on his claims of election fraud

item.title: Nearly half of Americans say they're worse off financially than a year ago
item.description: New York Fed survey shows growing concern about household finances and the job market, even as the job market remains solid.

item.title: Himes says Pulte in intelligence role is Trump's "worst and most dangerous" appointment
item.description: The top Democrat on the House Intelligence Committee said the timing of the appointment takes FISA Section 702 reauthorization "off the table."

item.title: Lawsuit attempts to stop UFC fight at White House on Trump's birthday
item.description: The lawsuit calls the event "deeply corrupt" and argues that it seeks to enrich the president and his allies and lacks proper authorization.

item.title: How an experimental weight-loss drug is being openly promoted by physicians
item.description: Doctors are jumping the gun to prescribe a medication lacking FDA approval that has gone viral on social media. "Why are we waiting?" one physician asked.

item.title: Treasury Department plans to use Iranian assets to help U.S. Gulf allies recover
item.description: The Treasury Department will use Iranian assets to help U.S. Gulf allies recover from damage caused by Tehran's regime, a source familiar with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's thinking told CBS News.
